compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The registry indicates P1-P4. Visually, only two containers are identifiable: a yellow pot (P1) and a black pot (P3). P2 and P4 are absent or entirely obscured/lost.

inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present (Black Pot with White Rabbit anchor)

plant_audit.P1: Soil surface appears dark and undisturbed; no visible foliage.
plant_audit.P3: Contains the white rabbit anchor; soil surface shows debris/white particulate matter. No healthy vegetative growth detected.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears consistently dark and potentially over-saturated or lacking organic cover.
biome_observations.anomalies: Introduction of a metal spoon (T-1) and a cylindrical white object (likely a cosmetic bottle or tube) in the current frame (CURRENT).
biome_observations.debris: White particulate matter (resembling perlite or eggshell fragments) present in P3.

temporal_deltas.t3_to_t2: Significant lighting drop; visual data degraded.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_t1: Introduction of a metal spoon into the yellow pot (P1).
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: Removal of the spoon; introduction of a white cylindrical object near the yellow pot.

visual_health_inference.reasoning: No active chlorophyll-bearing tissue is visible in any of the pots. The biome appears to be in a state of total plant loss, currently serving as a repository for non-botanical objects.

vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a complete absence of the expected botanical specimens. The environment has transitioned from a plant-tending station to a storage surface for household items. The soil in both visible pots is devoid of life, and the 'White Rabbit' anchor in P3 is the only remaining constant from the registry. The presence of foreign objects suggests the biome has been abandoned or repurposed.
